body_txt: The mysterious vanishing of a hero's established love interest between the first movie and the sequel. Related to the Cartwright Curse , but this is where what happened to her isn't mentioned , and is never referenced again. They can't have the hero settle down because Status Quo Is God . On the other hand, they can't make the hero look like a heel by spelling out why their relationships always fail, so the situation becomes the Elephant in the Living Room . Basically, the girls aren't put into the movies to be characters or interesting, but merely so that the main character can go through the motions of a fresh Token Romance in every single sequel. Related to Girl of the Week . Compare the Temporary Love Interest , who is taken far more seriously and gets the dignity of being written out, but again because adding a lasting romance would upset the status quo. See also Disposable Woman , Disposable Fiancé , and Chuck Cunningham Syndrome . Very common in a Sequel Reset . Written-In Absence and Sudden Sequel Death Syndrome are similarly related.
